description_long: This is a polygonal stone water well set on a flat, octagonal patch of ground. The well is constructed from stacked gray stone blocks forming a circular wall, topped with a wooden cover that fits snugly over the opening. A sturdy wooden frame with two vertical posts and a horizontal beam spans the well, supporting a metal rod for drawing water. A small metal bucket is attached to the rod, positioned above the well cover, suggesting a manual water retrieval system. The wood appears weathered and dark brown, while the stone is a medium gray with visible block segmentation. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and the well is isolated from any surrounding structures.
description: A stone water well with a wooden cover, supported by a wooden frame and equipped with a metal bucket for drawing water. The well sits on an octagonal patch of ground and has a simple, functional design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone well, wooden cover, bucket, and the full wooden frame with the metal rod clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view with the wooden post in the foreground, partially obscuring the well and bucket, and showing the thickness of the stone wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the wooden frame, the well cover, and the bucket positioned on top, with the rod extending across.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the wooden post centered, the bucket hanging on the rod, and the stone well wall visible behind.
attribute_well_cover_design: Flat, circular wooden cover made of planks, fits over the well opening, no visible hinges or handles.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A metal bucket is present, attached to a horizontal metal rod supported by the wooden frame.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a low stone wall, approximately 0.7 meters high, with a wooden frame above; no additional railing or canopy.
